steps.title: Identify the core demand
steps.content: The student's explicit goal—”provide an explanation and example”—signals the strongest evaluative criterion: an effective answer must state the law clearly AND provide a specific, concrete example that illustrates it.

steps.title: Apply that criterion
steps.content:
Choice D succeeds: it opens with a specific case (Europa's orbit is elliptical), names what the law describes, and clarifies the law's scope (applies to moons as well as planets).

The other three fail to accomplish both tasks:
  • A omits an example
  • B includes an example but contaminates it with irrelevant data (the orbital period)
  • C offers only a vague
, generic example instead of the concrete Europa case.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
